properties.description: Iconic 1.5km waterfront promenade along the Huangpu River with panoramic views of the Pudong skyline
properties.sustainability_notes: The Bund's 52 heritage buildings (mostly 1920s-30s) are protected under the Shanghai Municipal Heritage Conservation Plan; the 2010 waterfront renovation reclaimed 25m of road space from traffic to create a fully pedestrianised riverside promenade.
properties.notes: Best visited at sunset for skyline photos. Very crowded on weekends.

properties.description: Shanghai's iconic TV tower with observation decks and a glass floor at 259m
properties.sustainability_notes: High-energy-intensity tourist attraction with no documented mitigation measures; its primary role as a functioning broadcast tower slightly offsets the tourist-infrastructure energy cost.
properties.notes: Book tickets online to skip the queue.

properties.description: The tallest building in China at 632m, with the world's highest observation deck at 546m (119th floor) and a 360-degree panoramic view
properties.sustainability_notes: LEED Platinum certified (2016); 121 vertical-axis wind turbines on the upper floors supply approximately 1.2 GWh/year. The double-skin twisted glass facade reduces solar heat gain and wind load, cutting HVAC energy demand by 21% versus a conventional curtain wall (Gensler design data). Rainwater recycling system collects from the atrium cavity for grey-water reuse, reducing potable water consumption by an estimated 40%.
properties.notes: Book tickets in advance online. The elevator to the 119th floor is the world's fastest at 18m/s. Clear days offer views 100km out.

properties.description: 88-story Art Deco skyscraper inspired by traditional Chinese pagoda architecture, housing the Grand Hyatt hotel on upper floors
properties.sustainability_notes: Completed 1999; no major green certification documented. Pagoda-inspired tiered setbacks reduce wind tunnel effects at street level — a passive urban design benefit, though the building predates systematic sustainability reporting for Shanghai high-rises.
properties.notes: Observation deck on 88th floor. The Grand Hyatt atrium is worth seeing even if not staying. Good alternative if Shanghai Tower is sold out.

properties.description: 492m skyscraper with a distinctive trapezoid opening at the top; features a glass-floored sky walk observation deck at 474m
properties.sustainability_notes: The trapezoid void at the crown is a passive wind-pressure reduction feature rather than merely aesthetic, reducing structural steel requirements. No LEED or comparable green certification published; energy performance data not publicly available.
properties.notes: The 100th floor sky walk has transparent glass panels. Nicknamed 'the bottle opener' by locals. All three Lujiazui towers make a great combined visit.

properties.description: The open riverside promenade in Lujiazui offering the classic view back across the Huangpu River to The Bund — the best free vantage point for the Pudong skyline
properties.sustainability_notes: Free public riverfront park with zero entry infrastructure; the 2010 Lujiazui waterfront renewal added landscaped green verges and a continuous pedestrian path, improving non-motorised access along the Pudong riverfront.
properties.notes: Free alternative to paid observation decks. Walk the riverside path for changing angles on all three towers. Stunning at night when the towers are lit up.

properties.description: Shanghai's oldest steel bridge (1908), spanning the Suzhou Creek at its confluence with the Huangpu River — a beloved historic landmark and photo spot
properties.address: Waibaidu Bridge, Huangpu District (north end of The Bund)
properties.sustainability_notes: The 1908 steel Warren truss bridge was disassembled, repaired in a Jiangnan shipyard, and reinstated in 2009 — a restoration rather than a replacement, preserving 116-year-old structural fabric. Listed under Shanghai Municipal Heritage Protection.
properties.notes: Just a 5-minute walk north of The Bund. The view from the bridge looking south toward Pudong is excellent. Good for early-morning photography.

properties.description: Asia's newest Disney park opened 2016, featuring the largest Disney castle in the world (Enchanted Storybook Castle) and unique China-exclusive attractions
properties.address: Shendi Ring Rd, Pudong New Area
properties.sustainability_notes: Large-footprint theme park on previously agricultural Pudong land; Disney has published green building and waste-reduction targets for the Shanghai resort but third-party verified data on energy and water performance is not publicly available.
properties.notes: Reachable via Metro Line 11. Book tickets well in advance especially for weekends and holidays. Full day needed. The Zootopia land opened in 2023 is unique to Shanghai.

properties.description: 1,700-year-old water town on the outskirts of Shanghai with stone bridges, canal-side Ming and Qing dynasty architecture, and traditional snack streets
properties.sustainability_notes: National-level historic and cultural town (中国历史文化名镇) designation protects canal-side Ming and Qing streetscapes; the canal network is best explored on foot or by gondola, keeping the historic core car-free. Tourism commercialisation of snack streets has increased but permanent residents remain in core lanes.
properties.notes: About 40km west of central Shanghai; take Bus 1000 from Pu'an Rd or Metro Line 17 to Zhujiajiao station. Free to wander — individual temples and mansions charge 5-20 CNY each. Try the sticky rice dumplings (粽子).

properties.description: Shanghai's oldest and largest Buddhist temple complex, featuring a 40m-tall seven-story pagoda dating to 977 AD, active monastery with monks in residence
properties.sustainability_notes: The Longhua Pagoda (977 AD) is a national-level protected monument under NCHA (National Cultural Heritage Administration); the active monastic community maintains the complex under its own stewardship, limiting tourist-driven commercialisation relative to purely secular heritage sites.
properties.notes: Adjacent to Longhua Martyrs Memorial Cemetery. The temple complex is active — dress respectfully. Peach blossom festival in March draws large crowds.
